title: Huffman Coding
description: Huffman coding builds an optimal prefix-free code by repeatedly selecting the two lowest-frequency symbols and merging them into a new internal node. This greedy choice — always combining the lightest nodes — provably produces the shortest average code length for a given frequency distribution.

title: Job Scheduling
description: The Shortest Job First (SJF) scheduling algorithm greedily selects the process with the smallest burst time, minimizing average waiting time across all processes. This greedy strategy is provably optimal for non-preemptive scheduling when all jobs arrive simultaneously.

title: Coin Change in Vending Machines
description: Vending machines dispense change using a greedy algorithm: always give the largest denomination coin that does not exceed the remaining amount. For standard currency systems (where each denomination divides the next), this greedy approach provably produces the minimum number of coins.
